data.text/html:
Discourse Graph — Policy-Controlled Multi-Agent Knowledge Sharing

Domain: Lunar transfer stage propulsion trade study Agents: AliceGroup (systems architecture) · BobGroup (propulsion subsystem)

Layer stack

Layer Technology Purpose
Ontology OWL 2 DL (dg: + eng:) Class hierarchy and predicate semantics
Shapes SHACL Structural and relational constraints
Runtime Pydantic + rdflib Write-time validation and RDF storage
Policy RDF + SPARQL Formal, inspectable sharing rules

Five-act narrative

Act Cells Topic
1 2–5 Setup — load ontologies, create agents and graphs
2 6–11 Individual graphs — Alice and Bob build independently
3 12–17 Policy declaration — Alice writes two policies; inspect the Turtle and SPARQL artifacts
4 18–26 Sharing — policy A (direct evidence), policy B (hidden-backing claim); invariants demonstrated
5 27–28 Visualization and epistemic status summary

Two sharing policies

Policy Permitted set Epistemic character
evidence-sharing {E1} Direct evidence: Bob receives Alice's delta-V analysis with full provenance
arch-claim {C1} Hidden-backing claim: Bob receives Alice's baseline assertion with no visible justification
Ontology loaded: 109 triples (dg: + eng: merged)

Shapes loaded: 142 triples
Shape ID Constraint
QS-1 Question must have dg:content and rdfs:label
CS-1 Claim must have dg:content and rdfs:label
ES-1 Evidence must have dg:content and rdfs:label
ES-2 Evidence must support, oppose, or inform at least one node
SS-1 Source must have dg:content and rdfs:label
IS-1 IngestedNode must have prov:wasAttributedTo
DS-1 Decision must have eng:decision and eng:justification edges
AS-1 Assumption must have eng:assumptionScope
OP-1 eng:opens and eng:decision targets must be disjoint per Decision

Agents constructed:
  • alice_agent.uri = http://example.org/alice/agent
  • bob_agent.uri = http://example.org/bob/agent
  • alice_agent.graph_uri('local') = http://example.org/alice/graph/local
DiscourseGraph instances created:

AliceGroup graph populated — 7 nodes, 7 discourse edges.
ID Type Label
Q1 Question Q1-PropArch
C1 Claim C1-ChemBiprop
C2 Claim C2-SEPNotViable
E1 Evidence E1-DeltaV
E2 Evidence E2-Schedule
D1 Decision D1-SelectBiprop
Q2 Question Q2-ThrusterConfig (opened by D1)

D1 is connected to Q1 (eng:decision), C1 and E1 (eng:justification), and Q2 (eng:opens).</em></span></span></div>
BobGroup graph populated — 4 local nodes, 4 discourse edges.

<marimo-callout-output data-html='"<span class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"><span class=\"paragraph\"><strong>Bob (pre-sharing):</strong> CONFORMS ✓</span></span>"' data-kind='"success"'></marimo-callout-output>

<div style='display: flex;flex: 1;flex-direction: column;justify-content: flex-start;align-items: normal;flex-wrap: nowrap;gap: 0.5rem'><marimo-mime-renderer data-mime='"application/vnd.marimo+mimebundle"' data-data='"{\"image/png\": \"data:image/png;base64,\", \"__metadata__\": {\"image/png\": {\"width\": 790, \"height\": 589}}}"'></marimo-mime-renderer><span class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents"><span class="paragraph"><em>Bob's pre-sharing graph: Q2 (steelblue), C3 (seagreen), E3 (goldenrod), D2 (mediumpurple). D2 <code>eng:decision</code> bob_Q2 — Bob's subsystem question. Alice's E1 and C1 arrive in Act 4.</em></span></span></div>

Policy A declared: evidence-sharing
  • Grantee: http://example.org/bob/agent
  • Include types: [dg:Evidence]
  • Exclude nodes: [E2-Schedule]
  • Permitted set: {E1}
  • Policy URI: http://example.org/alice/policy/evidence-sharing

Policy A — RDF artifact (alice_dg._policy serialised as Turtle)

Alice wrote one Python call. The system produced this formal RDF. Note dg:SharingPolicy, dg:grantee, dg:sourceGraph, dg:includesType, dg:excludesNode.
@prefix ns1: <http://example.org/dg/1.0/> .
@prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .

<http://example.org/alice/policy/evidence-sharing> a ns1:SharingPolicy ;
    ns1:created "2026-03-19T20:11:00.235279+00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    ns1:excludesNode <http://example.org/alice/node/e2-schedule-7bd51872> ;
    ns1:grantee <http://example.org/bob/agent> ;
    ns1:includesType ns1:Evidence ;
    ns1:policyName "evidence-sharing"^^xsd:string ;
    ns1:sourceGraph <http://example.org/alice/graph/local> .

Policy A — compiled SPARQL CONSTRUCT

The SPARQL was generated from the RDF policy above — Alice never wrote SQL or SPARQL. The VALUES ?s clause scopes subjects to the permitted set. The FILTER implements the edge-bounding rule (FR-POL-9): a triple (s, p, o) is exported only if s ∈ permitted AND (isLiteral(o) OR p ∉ DISCOURSE_PREDICATES OR o ∈ permitted).
CONSTRUCT { ?s ?p ?o }
WHERE {
  GRAPH <http://example.org/alice/graph/local> {
    ?s ?p ?o .
    VALUES ?s { <http://example.org/alice/node/e1-deltav-41f5ee9c> }
    FILTER (
      isLiteral(?o)
      || ?p NOT IN (<http://example.org/dg/1.0/informs>, <http://example.org/dg/1.0/opposes>, <http://example.org/dg/1.0/supports>, <http://example.org/eng/1.0/decision>, <http://example.org/eng/1.0/justification>, <http://example.org/eng/1.0/opens>)
      || ?o IN (<http://example.org/alice/node/e1-deltav-41f5ee9c>)
    )
  }
}
Permitted set: {e1-deltav-41f5ee9c}

Policy B declared: arch-claim
  • Grantee: http://example.org/bob/agent
  • Include nodes: [C1-ChemBiprop]
  • Permitted set: {C1} — no type filter, no exclude
  • Policy URI: http://example.org/alice/policy/arch-claim

Policy graph after both declarations

@prefix ns1: <http://example.org/dg/1.0/> .
@prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .

<http://example.org/alice/policy/arch-claim> a ns1:SharingPolicy ;
    ns1:created "2026-03-19T20:11:00.363573+00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    ns1:grantee <http://example.org/bob/agent> ;
    ns1:includesNode <http://example.org/alice/node/c1-chembiprop-9c0791c9> ;
    ns1:policyName "arch-claim"^^xsd:string ;
    ns1:sourceGraph <http://example.org/alice/graph/local> .

<http://example.org/alice/policy/evidence-sharing> a ns1:SharingPolicy ;
    ns1:created "2026-03-19T20:11:00.235279+00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    ns1:excludesNode <http://example.org/alice/node/e2-schedule-7bd51872> ;
    ns1:grantee <http://example.org/bob/agent> ;
    ns1:includesType ns1:Evidence ;
    ns1:policyName "evidence-sharing"^^xsd:string ;
    ns1:sourceGraph <http://example.org/alice/graph/local> .
Both dg:SharingPolicy individuals are in the same isolated _policy graph. Contrast the dg:includesType triple in Policy A with the dg:includesNode triple in Policy B — different selection mechanisms, different epistemic characters.

Policy B — compiled SPARQL CONSTRUCT

Contrast with Policy A: VALUES ?s now contains only C1. No dg:supports or dg:informs edges point into {C1} from E1 or E2, so the edge-bounding FILTER drops all incoming discourse edges on C1.
CONSTRUCT { ?s ?p ?o }
WHERE {
  GRAPH <http://example.org/alice/graph/local> {
    ?s ?p ?o .
    VALUES ?s { <http://example.org/alice/node/c1-chembiprop-9c0791c9> }
    FILTER (
      isLiteral(?o)
      || ?p NOT IN (<http://example.org/dg/1.0/informs>, <http://example.org/dg/1.0/opposes>, <http://example.org/dg/1.0/supports>, <http://example.org/eng/1.0/decision>, <http://example.org/eng/1.0/justification>, <http://example.org/eng/1.0/opens>)
      || ?o IN (<http://example.org/alice/node/c1-chembiprop-9c0791c9>)
    )
  }
}
Permitted set: {c1-chembiprop-9c0791c9}
Policy A exported: 6 triples

E1 ingested into http://example.org/bob/graph/ingested-alice
  • Ingested graph triple count: 9

<marimo-callout-output data-html='"<span class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"><span class=\"paragraph\">Bob connects E1 directly: <code>E1 dg:informs alice_Q2</code> and <code>D2 eng:justification E1</code>. Evidence type is unchanged — an empirical finding is an empirical finding, regardless of provenance. Bob traces attribution via <code>prov:wasAttributedTo</code>.</span></span>"' data-kind='"info"'></marimo-callout-output>
Policy B exported: 6 triples
C1 ingested into http://example.org/bob/graph/ingested-alice
A1 created: http://example.org/bob/node/a1-bipropaccepted-3af7a5d5

D2 justification chain (complete):
Justification Origin Epistemic status
C3-DualEngine Bob's own Claim (local)
E3-SingleEngine Bob's own Evidence (local)
E1-DeltaV Alice via Policy A Evidence (ingested, full provenance)
A1-BipropAccepted Bob (derived from Alice's C1) Assumption (explicit scope)
AS-1 (Assumption scope required) passes — A1 has eng:assumptionScope. IS-1 (IngestedNode provenance) passes — E1 and C1 have prov:wasAttributedTo.

All triples in exported_A and exported_B were checked. Every discourse edge subject and object is in the respective permitted set. E2 does not appear in any discourse edge position.
alice_dg._policy is not alice_dg._store — asserted. The policy graph is never passed to _store.get_context(), _store.addN(), or any SPARQL query string. This isolation IS the enforcement mechanism.
eng:decision targets: {rdflib.term.URIRef('http://example.org/alice/node/q1-proparch-8e6ecfaf')} · eng:opens targets: {rdflib.term.URIRef('http://example.org/alice/node/q2-thrusterconfig-422ce966')} · Disjoint: ✓
isinstance(Assumption(...), Claim) is True — the Python class hierarchy mirrors the OWL subclass axiom.

<div style='display: flex;flex: 1;flex-direction: column;justify-content: flex-start;align-items: normal;flex-wrap: nowrap;gap: 0.5rem'><marimo-mime-renderer data-mime='"application/vnd.marimo+mimebundle"' data-data='"{\"image/png\": \"data:image/png;base64,\", \"__metadata__\": {\"image/png\": {\"width\": 1589, \"height\": 712}}}"'></marimo-mime-renderer><span class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents"><span class="paragraph"><em>Left: Alice's full graph — D1 connects Q1, C1, E1, and opens Q2. Right: Bob's post-sharing graph — E1* and C1* (dashed orange border = ingested), A1 (sandybrown = Assumption), dashed gray arrow = <code>prov:wasDerivedFrom</code> A1→C1*.</em></span></span></div>

Epistemic Status — BobGroup Post-Sharing

Node Type Origin Epistemic status
Q2-ThrusterConfig Question Alice (cross-agent ref) Shared URI — content known to Alice only
C3-DualEngine Claim Bob's own Local assertion
E3-SingleEngine Evidence Bob's own Local empirical finding
D2-DualEngine500N Decision Bob's own Local decision, justified below
E1-DeltaV Evidence Alice via Policy A Ingested empirical finding — full provenance, type unchanged
C1-ChemBiprop Claim Alice via Policy B Ingested isolated claim — evidence chain withheld by Alice's policy
A1-BipropAccepted Assumption Bob (derived from C1) Explicit assumption — scope declared, attribution preserved
Key observation: The two sharing policies produce two qualitatively different epistemic objects in Bob's graph. E1 arrives as a first-class empirical finding. C1 arrives as an isolated assertion; Bob's honest response is to promote it to an Assumption with explicit scope and attribution. The system enforces the boundary — Bob cannot see E1 or E2 behind C1 — and the Assumption makes that boundary visible in the graph structure itself.
